divergenceClassification.triggerCondition: Effective AGREE on ≥7 of 11 questions (was 7 of 10 in source v1.0; v3.1 adjusts for Q11)
divergenceClassification.qualityThreshold: Combined quality multiplier ≥ 0.7 on agreement-contributing questions
divergenceClassification.resolutionPath: No analyst intervention required for this state alone; standard methodology proceeds
divergenceClassification.output: ★★★ STRONG signal pattern + 0 contradiction records (per schema sheet 11)

divergenceClassification.highWeightCondition: AT LEAST ONE HIGH-weight pair-resolver AGREES
divergenceClassification.qualityThreshold: Combined quality multiplier ≥ 0.5 on agreement-contributing questions
divergenceClassification.resolutionPath: Co-presence note appended for diverging axes; one or more low-severity Contradiction records may issue

divergenceClassification.triggerCondition: AGREE on ≥5 non-governance axes AND DIVERGE on Axis 1 (Q1) and/or Axis 5 (Q5) AND R1 senior, R2 line-level
divergenceClassification.highWeightCondition: HIGH-weight pair-resolvers may agree or diverge — not determinative for this state
divergenceClassification.qualityThreshold: Quality multiplier ≥ 0.5 (role mismatch reduces it but does not invalidate)
divergenceClassification.resolutionPath: Senior respondent (R1) signal pattern is primary; junior respondent observation noted as operational layer
divergenceClassification.output: ★ WEAK signal pattern + SPLIT annotation + 1 medium-severity Contradiction record (cross_respondent_same_side)

divergenceClassification.triggerCondition: BOTH HIGH-weight pair-resolvers DIVERGE (e.g. R1=Q4-A R2=Q4-B AND R1=Q7-A R2=Q7-B for NT/STJ vs NT/STP pair)
divergenceClassification.qualityThreshold: Quality multiplier ≥ 0.5 on the failing questions (genuine divergence, not low-quality artifact)
divergenceClassification.resolutionPath: PRACTITIONER ESCALATION REQUIRED — within-pair distinction compromised
divergenceClassification.output: BLOCKED — no signal pattern issued + 1 high-severity Contradiction record (cross_respondent_same_side)

divergenceClassification.triggerCondition: R2 produces a third environment OUTSIDE the candidate pair (e.g. R1 → NT/STJ pair indicators; R2 → SFJ/SFP indicators)
divergenceClassification.highWeightCondition: Pair identification itself fails — pair-resolver weights inapplicable
divergenceClassification.resolutionPath: PRACTITIONER ESCALATION ONLY — adding R3 does NOT resolve. Per project rule: ④-B is irresolvable by additional respondents.
divergenceClassification.output: BLOCKED — no signal pattern issued + 1 critical-severity Contradiction record (cross_respondent_same_side, escalated_to_finding)

divergenceClassification.state:
PROJECT RULE (per user memory): "④-B (pair-identification failure) is governed by State ④-B logic. Adding a third respondent does not resolve ④-B; only practitioner escalation does. R3 option is valid only for ④-A (within-pair divergence)."

How to distinguish: examine R2's answer pattern. If R2's signals are scattered across the candidate pair (e.g. some NT/STJ, some NT/STP) → ④-A. If R2's signals concentrate on a DIFFERENT pair entirely (e.g. NT/STJ pair was hypothesised but R2 reads as SFJ/SFP) → ④-B.

Practitioner action: ④-A may be resolved by adding a third respondent who breaks the tie. ④-B requires practitioner re-diagnosis of the candidate pair itself — the original pair hypothesis was wrong.

contradictionOutput.routing: No analyst routing required from this comparator

contradictionOutput.recordsIssued: 0 to 3 records — one per materially-divergent question with quality multiplier ≥ 0.5
1 record covering Axis 1 / Axis 5 split, observedRoles populated with both R1 and R2 roles
contradictionOutput.routing: Standard analyst review queue; analyst applies methodology §3 Gate 4 override-permitted analysis
PRACTITIONER ESCALATION queue. Optionally: third respondent may be requested via methodology §5 escalation flow.
PRACTITIONER ESCALATION queue. Adding a third respondent does NOT resolve. Per project rule: only practitioner can resolve.

contradictionOutput.recordsIssued: May escalate to escalated_to_finding for ④-B
Identifies this comparator as the source

contradictionOutput.defaultSeverity: Both R1 and R2 answer IDs for the divergent question(s)

contradictionOutput.recordsIssued: Analyst transitions to under_review per methodology §5 state machine
For ④-B: pre-populated to indicate finding-grade escalation

triggerConditions.activationCondition: Highest weighted signal direction is identified but on a single-axis or single-question signal only. Combined evidence-quality multiplier (per Dual Respondent sheet 4 / per schema sheet 15) below the high-confidence gate threshold.
triggerConditions.meaning: A candidate signal pattern exists but evidence is too thin for analyst confirmation without verification. Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §3 Gate 3: confidence is capped at low until additional evidence is gathered.
triggerConditions.action:
→ Run accuracy check (sheet 2)
→ Select 35-Q instrument for the candidate (sheet 5)
→ Proceed to verification output (sheet 6)

triggerConditions.activationCondition: Two signal directions within 2 points (per EDv2 sheet 6_Layered_Scoring co-presence rule). Neither emerges as clearly dominant.
triggerConditions.meaning: Two environments co-present in the signal pattern. Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §1: this is NOT a "split type" finding — it is a signal pattern requiring analyst-mediated disambiguation.
triggerConditions.severity: medium to high (depending on which two)
triggerConditions.action:
→ Run accuracy check (sheet 2)
→ Select 35-Q instruments for BOTH candidates (sheet 5)
→ Run verification on both; signal pattern feeds analyst workspace

triggerConditions.activationCondition: Layer 1 instrument returned a partial differentiation — a signal direction emerged but coverage warning is active OR the signal is concentrated in only some questions of the relevant axis.
triggerConditions.meaning: Partial signal — instrument cannot fully discriminate without additional evidence.
triggerConditions.action:
→ Run accuracy check (sheet 2)
→ Select 35-Q instrument for the partial candidate (sheet 5)
→ Proceed to verification output (sheet 6)

triggerConditions.activationCondition: No positive signal direction emerged. Layer 1 questionnaire produced only "this is NOT environment X" eliminations without identifying a positive candidate.
triggerConditions.meaning: No candidate signal pattern. Engine cannot proceed to verification because there is nothing to verify.
triggerConditions.action:
→ Run accuracy check (sheet 2)
→ Do NOT serve a 35-Q instrument
→ Escalate to practitioner (sheet 7)

triggerConditions.activationCondition: Reliability-flag count from Answer records (schema sheet 7) exceeds the threshold per schema sheet 15_ConfidenceGate. Specifically: ≥40% of answers carry any reliability flag, OR any answer carries acquisition_framing_contamination, OR ≥3 answers carry status_protection / narrative_interest.
triggerConditions.meaning: Evidence reliability is too compromised for instrument-level verification to add value. Adding more questionnaire data will not improve confidence — the underlying respondent reliability is the limiting factor.
triggerConditions.action:
→ Run accuracy check (sheet 2)
→ DO NOT serve a 35-Q instrument (low ROI)
→ Route to analyst workspace via Contradiction record (sheet 4)
→ Practitioner consultation if no resolution path

triggerConditions.activationCondition: Contradiction count from cross-respondent comparator (Dual Respondent file) ≥ 5, OR ≥ 1 critical-severity Contradiction record, OR ④-B (pair-identification failure) state from Dual Respondent. Per methodology §5, ④-B is irresolvable by additional respondents.
triggerConditions.meaning: Cross-respondent or document evidence is structurally contradictory. Verification questionnaire will not resolve this — the disagreement is at the diagnostic level, not the instrument level.
triggerConditions.action:
→ Skip accuracy check (already failed implicitly)
→ DO NOT serve a 35-Q instrument
→ Route to analyst workspace via Contradiction Triage (methodology §5)
→ Practitioner escalation for ④-B cases

triggerConditions.trigger: Triage does NOT activate when ALL of the following hold: (a) Layer 1 produced a clear weighted signal pattern with combined evidence-quality multiplier ≥ 0.7 (per schema sheet 15 high-confidence gate); (b) reliability-flag count below threshold; (c) zero open Contradiction records; (d) no thin-coverage environment in primary or secondary signal. In this state: route directly to analyst workspace for standard review (no triage required).

Even when no other trigger fires, signals concentrated on thin-coverage environments require routing to dedicated 35-Q questionnaires per ST_Environment_Diagnostic_v2.xlsx sheet 5_Reliability_Audit:

 NT/STP (8/22 signals in EDv2) → NT_STP_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx
 NF/NT (8/22 signals in EDv2) → NF_NT_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx
 NF/SFP (3/22 signals in EDv2; relies on Q11 in AEM/TSAM) → NF_SFP_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx

For NF/SFP specifically: if Q11 returned both-F (cannot answer) from both respondents in dual-respondent comparison, NF/SFP cannot be ruled in or out by AEM/TSAM. Mandatory routing to NF_SFP questionnaire OR practitioner escalation.

accuracyChecks.questionToClient: Does the respondent who completed the diagnostic have at least 12 months of continuous observation of this organisation from their stated vantage point?
accuracyChecks.failureAction: If P2 = A (less than 12 months): respondent answers should be re-run with a respondent who has ≥ 12 months tenure. Send "tenure < 12 months" message template.
accuracyChecks.v31Update: Threshold unchanged from source v1.0. Note: AEM/TSAM v3.1 require 18 months for primary self-description, EDv2 v3.1 requires 12 months for external observation.

accuracyChecks.questionToClient: Did the respondent complete the diagnostic from a position that allows observation of authority mechanisms (Axis 1) and resource flows (Axis 5)?
accuracyChecks.failureAction: If P1 = C (external consultant) or P1 = B (mid-level manager) AND ambiguity is on Axis 1 or Axis 5: request a P1 = A senior internal respondent. Send "role = external or mid-level" message template.
accuracyChecks.v31Update: Preserved from source v1.0. Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §3 Gate 2 (Role Coverage): Tier-mandatory respondent gaps cap confidence at medium.

accuracyChecks.questionToClient: Each question in Level 1 is anchored to a specific recent observable event — not a description of general values or policy. Did the respondent answer based on what actually happened, or what they believe should happen?
accuracyChecks.failureAction: If client confirms answers were based on values/policy: re-administer Step 2-B with corrected instructions. Send "values-based answers" message template.
accuracyChecks.v31Update: Preserved from source v1.0. Per v3.1 architecture: this check overlaps with the Direct Observation Gate (added v3.1) — gate row should already filter values-based answers, but a respondent may bypass the gate.

accuracyChecks.questionToClient: Was the diagnostic completed by a single respondent or by 8–15 respondents per organisation as designed?
accuracyChecks.failureAction: ACQUIRER SIDE: route to dual-respondent comparator (single-respondent + recruit second respondent). TARGET SIDE: n=1 handling unchanged — single-respondent target self-assessment is acceptable but contamination flag mandatory.
accuracyChecks.v31Update: Preserved from April 2026 amendment. v3.1 update: target-side single-respondent now includes acquisition-framing contamination check by design (per Target Self-Assessment Module v3.1).

accuracyChecks.source: Per-question Direct Observation Gate (added v3.1) on AEM/TSAM/EDv2 questionnaires
accuracyChecks.questionToClient: Did the respondent answer YES to the Direct Observation Gate on the questions that drive the ambiguity? Or did they answer NO / document_supported?
accuracyChecks.failureAction: If gate is consistently NO across the ambiguity-driving questions: respondent does not have direct observation; route to alternative respondent OR document-based evidence. Confidence cap applies.
accuracyChecks.v31Update: NEW v3.1. Gate failure on the ambiguity-driving questions means instrument-level verification (35-Q) will not help — the same observation gap will recur.

accuracyChecks.source: Per-answer evidence-machinery: evidenceType, knowledgeLevel, confidence, reliabilityFlags
accuracyChecks.questionToClient: For the ambiguity-driving questions, what evidence type and knowledge level did the respondent declare? Were any reliability flags raised?
accuracyChecks.failureAction: If evidenceType = inference / hypothetical / unknown OR knowledgeLevel = speculative / not_known on > 50% of ambiguity-driving questions: instrument verification will not improve quality. Route to analyst workspace OR practitioner escalation.
accuracyChecks.v31Update: NEW v3.1. Required per ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx sheet 7_Answer_Record evidence-machinery fields.

accuracyChecks.check:
Run all six checks in sequence. Multiple failures may compound. Decision rules:

 • All 6 PASS → proceed to instrument selection (sheet 5)
 • Any 1-2 FAIL with corrective action available → send corrective message; re-run Layer 1; return to triage
 • Check 5 FAIL OR Check 6 FAIL → DO NOT serve 35-Q (instrument cannot fix the underlying problem); route to analyst workspace via Contradiction record (sheet 4) OR practitioner escalation
 • Any 3+ checks FAIL → practitioner escalation (sheet 7)
 • Client cannot provide corrective respondent → practitioner escalation (sheet 7)

reliabilityFlagTiers.trigger: Total flag rate < 10% of answers AND no critical flag
reliabilityFlagTiers.confidenceGateImpact: No impact — confidence may reach high if other gates pass

reliabilityFlagTiers.trigger: Total flag rate 10-40% OR moderate-tier triggers met
reliabilityFlagTiers.confidenceGateImpact: Confidence capped at medium (per schema sheet 15)
reliabilityFlagTiers.triageRouting: Analyst review with explicit reliability note in coverageNote
reliabilityFlagTiers.overridePermission: Analyst may override to flag specific findings as low confidence

reliabilityFlagTiers.trigger: Total flag rate > 40% OR high-tier triggers met OR single critical-tier instance
reliabilityFlagTiers.confidenceGateImpact: Confidence capped at low (per schema sheet 15)
reliabilityFlagTiers.triageRouting: Analyst review with mandatory contradicted_by_pattern flag on affected findings
reliabilityFlagTiers.overridePermission: Analyst SHOULD downgrade severity ratings on affected findings; analyst CAN issue follow_up_required label

reliabilityFlagTiers.trigger: ≥ 2 critical-tier instances OR pattern across multiple respondents
reliabilityFlagTiers.confidenceGateImpact: Confidence cannot exceed cannot_determine on RiskOutput aggregate
reliabilityFlagTiers.triageRouting: DO NOT issue paid output. Route to practitioner escalation. Findings may be issued as informational only with explicit reliability disclaimer.
reliabilityFlagTiers.overridePermission: Senior analyst sign-off required to issue any paid finding from this engagement

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Target-side respondent is aware of pending acquisition; answers may be distorted toward acceptability or self-protection.
reliabilityFlagRules.diagnosticWeight: CRITICAL — single instance forces high triage tier
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Auto-set when target-side respondent declares deal awareness in metadata
reliabilityFlagRules.routingImpact: Single instance → reliability tier HIGH; route to analyst workspace; document in coverageNote

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent answer protects role status, seniority, or organisational position.
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Detected when respondent answer is structurally implausible for their role (e.g. line-level claims of executive observation)

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent has personal narrative interest in the answer (founder describing own founding myth, etc.).
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Detected via cross-respondent triangulation: same-side respondents agree but differ from cross-side observers

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent has financial exposure to deal outcome (earn-out, retention, carry).
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Auto-set when respondent declares personal_economic_exposure = high in metadata

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent answered substantively but Direct Observation Gate was NO.
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Auto-set when gate row = no AND substantive option selected (not E)

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent answered based on observation > 24 months old.
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Detected when knowledgeLevel = pattern_based AND observation tenure marker indicates older memory

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent answer aligns with same-side peer answers but contradicts documentary evidence.
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Detected via document divergence: peer-aligned answer differs from verified EvidenceItem

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent answered questions outside their legitimate access scope (per schema sheet 14_RoleCoverageMatrix).
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Auto-set when respondent role is below-tier for the question scope (e.g. line-level on Axis 1)

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Respondent gave self-contradictory answers within their own questionnaire.
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Detected by internal consistency check (e.g. gate=no but substantive option selected on same question)
reliabilityFlagRules.routingImpact: ≥ 1 instance → reliability tier MODERATE; ≥ 2 → HIGH; pattern across answers may invalidate the entire respondent set per methodology §5

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Total flag rate < 10% of answers AND no critical flag
reliabilityFlagRules.diagnosticWeight: No impact — confidence may reach high if other gates pass

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Total flag rate 10-40% OR moderate-tier triggers met
reliabilityFlagRules.diagnosticWeight: Confidence capped at medium (per schema sheet 15)
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Analyst review with explicit reliability note in coverageNote
reliabilityFlagRules.routingImpact: Analyst may override to flag specific findings as low confidence

reliabilityFlagRules.description: Total flag rate > 40% OR high-tier triggers met OR single critical-tier instance
reliabilityFlagRules.diagnosticWeight: Confidence capped at low (per schema sheet 15)
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: Analyst review with mandatory contradicted_by_pattern flag on affected findings
reliabilityFlagRules.routingImpact: Analyst SHOULD downgrade severity ratings on affected findings; analyst CAN issue follow_up_required label

reliabilityFlagRules.description: ≥ 2 critical-tier instances OR pattern across multiple respondents
reliabilityFlagRules.diagnosticWeight: Confidence cannot exceed cannot_determine on RiskOutput aggregate
reliabilityFlagRules.triggers: DO NOT issue paid output. Route to practitioner escalation. Findings may be issued as informational only with explicit reliability disclaimer.
reliabilityFlagRules.routingImpact: Senior analyst sign-off required to issue any paid finding from this engagement

contradictionTiers.triageRouting: Standard analyst review path; no triage routing required from this dimension

contradictionTiers.trigger: 1-2 open Contradiction records, all severity ≤ medium
contradictionTiers.triageRouting: Standard analyst review queue; analyst applies §5 triage matrix per record
contradictionTiers.perStAnalystMethodologyXlsx: §5 standard procedure: open → under_review → resolved or escalated_to_finding

contradictionTiers.trigger: 3-4 open Contradiction records OR ≥ 1 high-severity record
contradictionTiers.triageRouting: Priority analyst review queue; senior analyst review preferred. Confidence Gate (§3 Gate 3) impact applied.
contradictionTiers.perStAnalystMethodologyXlsx: §5 with senior analyst preference. Confidence capped at medium until contradictions resolved.

contradictionTiers.trigger: ≥ 5 open records OR ≥ 1 critical-severity record OR ④-B (pair-identification failure) state from Dual Respondent comparator
contradictionTiers.triageRouting: PRACTITIONER ESCALATION REQUIRED. Do not issue paid output until contradictions resolved.
contradictionTiers.perStAnalystMethodologyXlsx: §5 with mandatory practitioner consultation. ④-B: per documented project rule, additional respondents do NOT resolve — only practitioner can re-diagnose pair hypothesis.

contradictionTiers.tier:
When BOTH dimensions activate, the combined tier is the more severe of the two — not an average. Examples:

 • Reliability tier MODERATE + Contradiction tier FEW → effective tier MODERATE
 • Reliability tier MODERATE + Contradiction tier MANY → effective tier MANY (higher of the two)
 • Reliability tier HIGH + Contradiction tier NONE → effective tier HIGH
 • Reliability tier CRITICAL + Contradiction tier ANY → effective tier CRITICAL/BLOCKING
 • Reliability tier ANY + Contradiction tier BLOCKING → effective tier BLOCKING

Rationale: a chain is as reliable as its weakest link. Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §6 Risk Output Composition Procedure: confidence is the MINIMUM across constituent assessments, not the average.

instrumentSelection.candidateSignalDirection: Both candidate signal directions (ENV_1 and ENV_2)
instrumentSelection.administeredTo: Same respondent group completes both (different sessions preferred)

instrumentSelection.instrumentFileToServe: DO NOT SERVE a 35-Q instrument — reliability is the limiting factor, not coverage
instrumentSelection.v31Note: Route to analyst workspace (sheet 4 routing) OR practitioner

instrumentSelection.instrumentFileToServe: DO NOT SERVE a 35-Q instrument — contradictions are at diagnostic level, not instrument level
instrumentSelection.v31Note: Route to analyst workspace via methodology §5 OR practitioner for ④-B

instrumentSelection.trigger: When the co-presence signal involves one of the four critical pairs from EDv2 Level 2 Deepening, the 35-Q instruments below are the targeted resolution. Use this table to confirm the right instrument(s).
Pair A (Power Racket vs Enforcer Network)
Pair B (Hometown Network vs Franchise Machine)
Pair C (Performance Arena vs Disruption Lab)
Pair D (Mission Field vs Idea Lab)
Pair E (Creative Commons standalone — v3.1 NEW)
instrumentSelection.v31Note: No paired counterpart; serve solo when Q11-C signals NF/SFP and confirmation needed

instrumentSelection.trigger: The 35-Q instrument filenames use underscore form (e.g. SFP_SFJ_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx) because they were saved as filenames in pre-v3.1 work and the strict file-renaming rule prevents renaming. The environment code itself is canonical SFP/SFJ (slash form). Filenames with underscores reference instruments; environment codes in content always use slash form.

cells.1: VERIFICATION OUTPUT · How to read 35-Q instrument results · v3.1

The 35-Q instruments produce signal patterns. v3.1 architectural change: the output is NOT a final environment label. It is a signal pattern that feeds analyst review. Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §1, no Layer 1 instrument (including 35-Q verification) names a final environment in a paid output.

cells.3: Notes

cells.2: 35 questions · 7 thematic sections · 5 questions per section
25 points per section · 175 maximum per respondent
cells.3: Section-level granularity
These instruments have not been v3.1-rebuilt. Their output is treated as a signal pattern in v3.1, not as a final environment lock.
Standard administration parameters

cells.2: Score all respondents on same instrument. Composite signal = majority pattern across respondents. If split 4/4: request 2 additional respondents before classifying.
NF_NT, NF_SFJ, NF_SFP, NT_STJ, NT_STP, SFJ_SFP, SFP_SFJ, STJ_STP, STP_STJ
cells.3: Each as Diagnostic_Questionnaire (practitioner) and Branded_Questionnaire (client-facing)
Triage action

cells.2: Verification candidate signal pattern is consistent with the [ENV] hypothesis. Not a final environment label.
cells.3: EXIT triage. Route to analyst workspace with confirmed candidate signal pattern. Analyst applies §4 procedure to author findings.
cells.4: ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §4 (Analyst Assessment authoring) — analyst issues findings citing this signal pattern as supporting evidence.

cells.2: Verification did NOT fully confirm the candidate. Signal direction remains ambiguous.
cells.3: Check whether co-presence pair returned CONFIRMED on the OTHER candidate. If yes: that direction is the elimination outcome. If no: route to practitioner.
cells.4: §4 evidenceLabel = follow_up_required permitted with explicit gap description.

cells.2: Two signal directions remain co-present after targeted verification. NOT a single primary.
cells.3: Do NOT attempt algorithmic resolution. Route to analyst workspace with both signal directions. Co-presence is itself diagnostic information.
cells.4: §5 contradictionType = answer_vs_pattern. Co-presence is a Contradiction record candidate.

cells.2: Verification eliminated the candidate. Candidate is NOT present in target organisation.
cells.3: If co-presence pair: serve OTHER candidate 35-Q instrument. If no other candidate: route to practitioner.
cells.4: §4 elimination is itself a finding — issue an Analyst Assessment recording the elimination.

cells.1: Source v1.0 had "LOCK the environment code. Exit triage framework." This contradicts ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §1: no Layer 1 instrument may produce a final environment label as the deliverable. v3.1 replaces this with: "EXIT triage. Route to analyst workspace with confirmed candidate signal pattern." The analyst then composes the final Risk Output records (per §6 of methodology). Environment classifications inform the analyst; they are NOT the deliverable.
PRACTITIONER ESCALATION · Conditions and brief contents · v3.1

Triggered when algorithmic triage cannot resolve. Engine responsibility ends here; practitioner takes over via 90-minute qualitative session. v3.1 additions: ④-B pair-identification failure (per documented project rule), reliability tier CRITICAL, contradiction tier BLOCKING, evidence-machinery quality failures.

cells.3: Practitioner action
90-min qualitative session. Practitioner reviews both 35-Q outputs + raw respondent answers + reliability flags.

cells.1: 35-Q returned NOT_[ENV] and no other candidate exists
cells.3: 90-min session to identify what positive signal pattern (if any) is observable from documents and qualitative interview.

cells.1: 35-Q returned MIXED with new third environment
cells.3: 90-min session to determine whether the third environment is genuinely co-present or an instrument artifact.

cells.1: Accuracy check failed and client cannot supply corrective respondent
cells.3: 90-min session. Practitioner determines whether engagement can proceed with available evidence or requires scope reduction.

cells.1: Client re-submitted Step 2-B 3+ times without resolving ambiguity
cells.3: 90-min session. Repeated re-administration producing same result is itself diagnostic — likely systematic response bias OR genuinely complex dual-environment structure.

cells.1: ④-B (pair-identification failure) from Dual Respondent comparator (v3.1 NEW)
cells.3: Per documented project rule: ④-B is irresolvable by additional respondents. Only practitioner can re-diagnose the pair hypothesis. Adding R3 does NOT resolve.
90-min session before issuing any paid output. Practitioner determines whether engagement is salvageable or requires rescoping.
90-min session. Practitioner applies methodology §5 with full evidence access and context the engine cannot synthesise.

cells.1: Evidence-machinery quality < 0.4 across majority of answers (v3.1 NEW)
cells.3: 90-min session. Engagement may require additional respondents OR document-based evidence augmentation OR scope reduction.

decisionTree.conditionAction: Layer 1 questionnaires complete. Read weighted signal pattern from EDv2 sheet 6_Layered_Scoring + AEM/TSAM 7_Layered_Scoring. Read reliability flags from Answer records. Read open Contradiction records from comparator.
decisionTree.exitConditions:
→ All quality gates pass + 0 contradictions: EXIT TRIAGE; route to analyst workspace for standard review.
→ Any trigger condition fires: ENTER TRIAGE (step 2 below).

decisionTree.conditionAction:
TRIAGE ENTERED. Identify which of six trigger conditions fired (sheet 1):
TRIGGER 1 weak signal · TRIGGER 2 co-presence · TRIGGER 3 partial · TRIGGER 4 negative-only · TRIGGER 5 reliability saturation (v3.1) · TRIGGER 6 contradiction blocking (v3.1)
decisionTree.exitConditions:
→ TRIGGER 6 BLOCKING: Skip to Step 7 (practitioner escalation).
→ TRIGGER 4 NEGATIVE-ONLY: Skip to Step 7.
→ TRIGGER 5 SATURATION: Skip Step 4 (do not serve 35-Q); proceed to Step 6.
→ TRIGGERS 1, 2, 3: Proceed to Step 3.

decisionTree.conditionAction: Run Accuracy Check (sheet 2). All 6 checks: Tenure · Role · Event-anchored answers · Multiple respondents · Gate pattern (v3.1) · Evidence-machinery quality (v3.1).
decisionTree.exitConditions:
→ Checks 1/2/3/4 fail with corrective respondent available: send corrective message; re-run Layer 1; return to Step 1.
→ Check 5 OR Check 6 fail: skip Step 4 (instrument cannot fix); proceed to Step 6.
→ 3+ checks fail: Step 7.
→ All pass: Step 4.

decisionTree.conditionAction: Select 35-Q instrument(s) per Sheet 5. Send client message. Await responses from 8-15 respondents.
decisionTree.exitConditions:
→ Responses received: Step 5.
→ Client cannot provide 8-15 respondents: Step 7 with scope-reduction note.

decisionTree.conditionAction: 35-Q responses received. Read aggregated EnvironmentFlag from instrument(s).
decisionTree.exitConditions:
→ CONFIRMED: Step 6 with confirmed candidate signal pattern.
→ PARTIAL (one CONFIRMED, other PARTIAL): Step 6 with CONFIRMED pattern.
→ MIXED: Step 6 with co-present signal pattern.
→ NOT_[ENV] in co-presence pair: serve OTHER 35-Q (return to Step 4).
→ All NOT_[ENV] / both PARTIAL / MIXED with new third env: Step 7.

decisionTree.conditionAction: CANDIDATE SIGNAL PATTERN IDENTIFIED. Apply combined reliability + contradiction tier matrix (sheet 4).
decisionTree.exitConditions:
→ Combined tier ≤ MODERATE: route to standard analyst review queue per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §4.
→ Combined tier MANY: priority analyst review, senior analyst preferred.
→ Combined tier HIGH/CRITICAL/BLOCKING: Step 7.
→ EXIT TRIAGE on routing.

decisionTree.conditionAction: PRACTITIONER ESCALATION (sheet 7). Compile practitioner brief. Send escalation message to client.
decisionTree.exitConditions:
→ Engine role complete.
→ Practitioner schedules 90-minute session.
→ Practitioner output re-enters at analyst workspace as findings or scope adjustment.

decisionTree.step: WHAT THIS DECISION TREE DOES NOT DO

This decision tree does NOT lock environment codes. It does NOT exit to "environment code resolved" or "environment code locked." Its terminal states are either: (a) routing to analyst workspace with a signal pattern + reliability/contradiction tier annotations; or (b) routing to practitioner. The analyst or practitioner makes the final call. Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §1: no Layer 1 instrument names a final environment as the deliverable.
